rejected_candidates.title: Any dust withdrawal can indefinitely censor emergency exits for all users in a token pool
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of F-001. The existing finding is stronger because `withdraw(token, 0)` already suffices, so a dust withdrawal variant adds no new reportable issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded mainnet token and Compound addresses make cross-chain deployments unsafe
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a deployment/configuration footgun rather than an intrinsic vulnerability in the audited deployment. The code is clearly written for a specific mainnet address set, and the issue materializes only if operators intentionally deploy it in an unsupported environment.
